artifact.content_preview:
From: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Site A AD Migration — Progress Update
Date: March 6, 2026

Alice,

Quick update on the Active Directory migration preparation for Site A:

1. Domain Controller Staging: The two new DC servers at Site A have been
   racked, powered on, and joined to the test domain. Windows Server
   patching is complete (all critical patches as of March 1 applied).

2. Schema Extension Testing: The AD schema extensio

extracted_tasks.text: will begin the production DC staging activities next
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: will begin the production DC staging activities next

metadata.subject: Site A AD Migration — Progress Update

risks.description: Critical hardware dependency: The migration relies on the delivery and installation of Catalyst 9500 network switches. Any delay in shipping or installation will halt final connectivity testing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The only dependency is the network switch delivery — we need the Catalyst 9500s in place before we can do final network connectivity testing for the DCs.

risks.description: Tight schedule buffer: There is only a four-day buffer between the planned completion of staging (March 14) and the hard migration window (March 18). Any technical issues during staging will likely push the Go-Live date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: targeting completion by March 14 to give us four days of buffer before the March 18 migration window.

risks.description: Legacy GPO Migration Complexity: Consolidating 47 legacy GPOs from different sites (B and C) into Site A carries a risk of unforeseen policy conflicts or 'ghost' settings that may not have been captured in the manual review.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I've reviewed the 47 legacy GPOs from Sites B and C. 23 are duplicates that can be deleted. 24 unique GPOs will be migrated.

risks.description: Patching Gap: Servers were patched as of March 1. If the migration occurs on March 18, there is a risk of 'Patch Tuesday' (typically the second Tuesday of the month, March 10) introducing new critical vulnerabilities or required updates that haven't been applied or tested.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Windows Server patching is complete (all critical patches as of March 1 applied).

risks.description: Single Point of Failure in Review: The migration script and security compliance review were performed by a single individual (Dave Chen). This represents a resource/knowledge silo risk if the script fails during production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The migration script is ready and has been reviewed by Dave Chen for security policy compliance.

risks.description: Rollback Downtime: While the rollback procedure is tested, a 45-minute rollback time plus the time taken to identify a failure could exceed the maintenance window, leading to service outages for Site A.

evidence_refs.excerpt: estimated rollback time is 45 minutes from Go-Live.

risks.description: Environment Disparity: Schema extensions were successful in 'test,' but production environments often have latent metadata or lingering objects not present in test, which can cause schema failures during the promotion to staging/production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The AD schema extension ran successfully in the test environment last night... Ready to promote to staging this weekend.
